nes_activity_strength_score_excerpt: In the 1+ to 9+ scoring system for NES activity, the BRCA1 NES was rated as 5+.
nes_binding_receptor_excerpt: facilitates export via the CRM1/exportin pathway | mediates CRM1-dependent export of BRCA1 | CRM1 nuclear export receptor
nes_conservation_status_excerpt: The large hydrophobic amino acids in the BRCA1 export sequence that conform to the consensus NES spacing are well conserved between species
nes_critical_hydrophobic_residues_excerp: Mutation of Leu 86 and Ile 90 (mutant m1) or Phe 93 and Leu 95 (mutant m2) to alanine completely abrogated export activity | NES-inactivating alanine substitutions of Leu 86 and Ile 90
nes_definition: Nuclear export signals are short peptides containing a series of closely spaced large hydrophobic residues, often leucines, that facilitate protein export from the nucleus via the CRM1/exportin pathway.
nes_definition_excerpt: The Rev-type NESs are short peptides containing a series of closely spaced large hydrophobic residues, often leucines | facilitates export via the CRM1/exportin pathway
nes_functional_outcome: Facilitates nuclear export of BRCA1, leading to its cytoplasmic localization; inactivation or inhibition of the NES induces nuclear accumulation of BRCA1.
nes_functional_outcome_excerpt: facilitates export via the CRM1/exportin pathway | Mutational inactivation of this nuclear export sequence... induced nuclear accumulation of ectopic full-length BRCA1 | results in the nuclear accumulation of BRCA1 | promoted the nuclear exit of the otherwise export-deficient chimeric protein Rev(1.4)-GFP | Inactivation of the BRCA1 NES increased the nuclear localization of the fusion protein in all cell lines.
nes_hydrophobic_spacing_pattern_excerpt: The BRCA1 NES contains a series of large hydrophobic amino acids with a 3:2:1 spacing | the 3:2:1 spacing of the critical hydrophobic residues is analogous to that seen in other highly active export sequences
nes_identification_method: Deletion analysis and a Rev(1.4)-GFP nuclear export assay.
nes_identification_method_excerpt: identified a functional Rev-type nuclear export sequence... by using a combination of deletion analysis and a recently developed nuclear export assay (22) | We next tested BRCA1 amino acid fragments 1–70 and 65–170 for export activity, using a new assay that enables identification of active NESs (22).
nes_leptomycin_b_sensitivity: Yes, nuclear export mediated by the NES is inhibited by Leptomycin B (LMB).
nes_leptomycin_b_sensitivity_excerpt: treatment of cells with the CRM1-specific export inhibitor leptomycin B, induced nuclear accumulation of ectopic full-length BRCA1 | a shift that was fully blocked by LMB (data not shown)

nes_regulatory_mechanism: Activity might be altered by post-translational modifications such as phosphorylation or after DNA damage, or by masking upon binding of BRCA1 to other proteins near the RING finger motif.
nes_regulatory_mechanism_excerpt: the activity of the NES might be altered by post-translational modifications such as phosphorylation, which BRCA1 undergoes in a cell cycle-dependent fashion... or after DNA damage | the proximity of the NES to the BRCA1 RING finger motif... points to the possibility that the NES could become masked upon binding of BRCA1 to other proteins.

nes_binding_receptor_excerpt: CRM1 is the major nuclear export receptor. | CRM1 is the most-prominent nuclear export receptor. It transports hundreds of different proteins harboring nuclear export signals (NESs) out of the nucleus
nes_definition: Nuclear export signals (NESs) are motifs found in hundreds of different proteins that enable their transport out of the nucleus by the CRM1 receptor.
nes_definition_excerpt: CRM1 is the most-prominent nuclear export receptor. | It transports hundreds of different proteins harboring nuclear export signals (NESs) out of the nucleus
nes_functional_outcome: Mediation of protein transport out of the nucleus, such as the nuclear export of GFP-NFAT.
nes_functional_outcome_excerpt: It transports hundreds of different proteins harboring nuclear export signals (NESs) out of the nucleus | wild-type and mutant CRM1 supported nuclear export of GFP-NFAT to very similar extents
nes_leptomycin_b_sensitivity: Yes, nuclear export mediated by CRM1 (the NES receptor) is inhibited by Leptomycin B (LMB).
nes_leptomycin_b_sensitivity_excerpt: Permeabilized GFP-NFAT cells were pre-treated in transport buffer with an ATP-regenerating system... and 100 nM LMB in a 30°C water bath for 15 min to remove soluble transport factors and block endogenous CRM1.
